env.name: POSTGRES_USER
env.label: POSTGRES_USER
env.description: Username for the default PostgreSQL user

env.name: POSTGRES_PASSWORD
env.label: POSTGRES_PASSWORD
env.description: Password for the default PostgreSQL user

env.name: POSTGRES_DB
env.label: POSTGRES_DB
env.description: Database name for the default PostgreSQL user

env.name: DB_NAME
env.label: DB_NAME
env.description: Comma or space separated list of PostgreSQL databases to backup

env.name: SCHEDULE
env.label: SCHEDULE
env.description: Cron schedule specifying the interval between PostgreSQL backups. Defaults to @daily

note: You must create the pgadmin folder manually and run `sudo chown 5050:5050 pgadmin` otherwise it will not work
description: PostgreSQL, also known as Postgres, is a free and open-source relational database management system that emphasizes extensibility and SQL compliance. This stack includes pgAdmin4 for web-based management and PGBackups for scheduled backups.
name: pgsql-pgadmin-pgbackups
title: PostgreSQL, pgAdmin4, and PGBackups
